summary: Iteration 20 verification: tested (1) zero 'emergent' substring in 7 public pages under prod Host header, (2) subscription plan icons render from local /assets/images/subscriptions/plan-{1..4}.png (all 4 < 50KB, image/png, HTTP 200), (3) DB scrubbed (subscription_plans=0, email_templates=0, email_outbox pending=0 emergent rows), (4) gzip Content-Encoding active on /, /shop.php, /product.php?slug=windows-11-pro with >50% compression ratio, (5) sitemap.xml XSLT-styled + 152 <url> entries + max lastmod within last 7 days, (6) iter-17/18/19 regressions all green (except 1 pre-existing preview-route flake on /assets/sitemap.xsl unrelated to this iteration). 23/23 new iter-20 tests pass; 99/101 regression tests pass.

minor.issue: python-requests HTTP/1.1 GET returns 502 Cloudflare Bad Gateway intermittently for ONLY this one asset path. curl HTTP/2 and internal origin (http://127.0.0.1:3000) both serve 200 OK with correct application/xml + table headers. Affects test_iter18_bing_feed_sitemap.py::TestSitemapRendering::test_xsl_asset_reachable + test_xsl_table_headers_present. NOT caused by iter-20 changes — sitemap.xml itself works fine on the same preview URL. Likely Cloudflare edge cache/origin-routing quirk for that single static path.

critical_code_review_comments: Iter-20 fix is correctly scoped: under Host=maventechsoftware.com, 0 'emergent' references on /, /subscriptions.php, /product.php?slug=windows-11-pro, /about-us.php, /shop.php, /checkout.php, /contact.php.

Subscription plan icons successfully migrated from https://static.prod-images.emergentagent.com/... to /assets/images/subscriptions/plan-{1..4}.png. File sizes 18.4-25.3 KB (down from ~1 MB; 98% reduction confirmed).

Gzip via ob_gzhandler in router.php works: Content-Encoding: gzip header set on /, /shop.php, /product.php; observed compression ratios ~80% (118KB->22KB on /). Verified via both header check and wire-size measurement (curl --compressed).
Sitemap remains XSLT-styled (152 <url> entries, max lastmod within 24h), confirming dynamic regeneration on each request.

Note: when the same pages are served via the Cloudflare preview proxy (Host: show-live-7.preview.emergentagent.com), the HTML legitimately includes the preview hostname in canonical/og:url/nav links (12 occurrences); this is expected and unrelated to the legacy CDN reference that was scrubbed. The production-host requirement is fully satisfied.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (subscriptions page renders all 4 local plan icons at 256x256 natural size)

test_credentials: admin@maventechsoftware.com / Admin@UC2026! (used by existing public_domain_hygiene tests; not needed for iter-20 tests)
seed_data_creation: None — all tests read existing state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-20 file is /app/backend/tests/test_iter20_emergent_scrub_gzip_sitemap.py — uses Host: maventechsoftware.com against http://127.0.0.1:3000 to verify production-host rendering. Plan icons are at /app/php-version/assets/images/subscriptions/plan-{1..4}.png. DB cleanups verified via direct mysql -uroot ucode_store SELECT COUNT(*) queries. The 2 failing iter-18 tests (test_xsl_asset_reachable, test_xsl_table_headers_present) hit the EXTERNAL preview URL via python-requests for /assets/sitemap.xsl which intermittently 502s through Cloudflare for ONLY that asset path while sitemap.xml itself returns 200 — this is preview-infra flakiness, not an iter-20 regression; origin serves XSL fine.
rca_of_the_issue: User reported 3 items: (1) emergent links visible on production deploy, (2) slow site, (3) sitemap update. Main agent fixed all three: source-edited subscription icon_image URLs in /app/php-version/includes/subscriptions.php from emergent CDN to local paths, added 4 compressed PNG icons (~20KB each), enabled ob_gzhandler in router.php for site-wide gzip, and confirmed sitemap.xml is dynamically generated each request. All 3 bug fixes verified working under the production Host header.
